'''Jack Charles''' (born 30 January 2006) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sh}} or {{rlp|so}} for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Betfred Super League]].

He previously played for [[Hull FC]] in the [[Super League]] and spent time on loan from [[Hull FC|Hull]] at [[Doncaster RLFC|Doncaster]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>[https://www.itsrugby.co.uk/players/jack-charles-61042.html itsrugby profile]</ref>

==Background==
Charles was born in [[Salford, Greater Manchester]], England. His father [[Chris Charles]] is a former professional rugby league footballer.<ref>[https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/im-rugby-league-nerd-jack-9274480 'I'm a rugby league nerd' Jack Charles shares love for Hull FC and sport as young half sets lofty ambitions]</ref>

He played for the [[Beverley Braves]] as a junior.<ref>[https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/nextgen-jack-charles-one-of-the-jewels-in-hull-fcs-academy-crown NextGen: Jack Charles, one of the jewels in Hull FC’s academy crown]</ref>

Charles joined [[Hull FC|FC]]'s Academy system, and played in their under 18s side in the 2023 season.<ref>[https://www.hullfc.com/blog/2024/06/12/charles-signs-two-year-contract-extension/ Charles Signs Two-Year Contract Extension]</ref>

He played for the Yorkshire Origin side and the England Academy side.<ref>[https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/4274/fc-youngster-charles-commits-to-club FC youngster Charles commits to club]</ref><ref>[https://www.hullfc.com/blog/2024/06/12/charles-hutchinson-named-in-england-academy-squad/ Charles & Hutchinson Named In England Academy Squad]</ref>

==Career==
Charles made his professional debut for [[Hull FC]] in March 2024 against the [[London Broncos]] in the [[2024 Super League season|Super League]].<ref>[https://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/p0hx91j9 Jack Charles ahead of London Broncos]</ref>

He spent time on loan from [[Hull FC|Hull]] at [[Doncaster RLFC|Doncaster]] in the [[2025 RFL Championship]].<ref>[https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/jack-charles-career-first-hull-1110343 Jack Charles' career first as Hull FC advice and Mikey Lewis motivation laid out]</ref>

Charles joined arch rivals [[Hull Kingston Rovers|Hull KR]] on a four-year deal ahead of the [[2026 Super League season]].<ref>[https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2l82l0047o Charles crosses city to join Hull KR]</ref>
